title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. nevadensis
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Nevada daffodil or Spanish daffodil, is a subspecies of the wild daffodil, a perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Pyrenees. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Nevada daffodil include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and aid in wound healing, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into tinctures, infusions, or salves, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
